speaker:
MASTER
               
              GUNNER
text: Sirrah, thou know’st how Orleance is besieged

speaker:
MASTER
               
              GUNNER
text: And how the English have the suburbs won.

speaker: BOY
text: Father, I know, and oft have shot at them;

speaker: BOY
text: Howe’er, unfortunate, I missed my aim.

speaker:
MASTER
               
              GUNNER
text: But now thou shalt not. Be thou ruled by me.

speaker:
MASTER
               
              GUNNER
text: Chief master-gunner am I of this town;

speaker:
MASTER
               
              GUNNER
text: Something I must do to procure me grace.

speaker:
MASTER
               
              GUNNER
text: The Prince’s espials have informèd me

speaker:
MASTER
               
              GUNNER
text: How the English, in the suburbs close entrenched,

speaker:
MASTER
               
              GUNNER
text: Went through a secret grate of iron bars

speaker:
MASTER
               
              GUNNER
text: In yonder tower, to overpeer the city,

speaker:
MASTER
               
              GUNNER
text: And thence discover how with most advantage

speaker:
MASTER
               
              GUNNER
text: They may vex us with shot or with assault.

speaker:
MASTER
               
              GUNNER
text: To intercept this inconvenience,

speaker:
MASTER
               
              GUNNER
text: A piece of ordnance ’gainst it I have placed,

speaker:
MASTER
               
              GUNNER
text: And even these three days have I watched

speaker:
MASTER
               
              GUNNER
text: If I could see them. Now do thou watch,

speaker:
MASTER
               
              GUNNER
text: For I can stay no longer.

speaker:
MASTER
               
              GUNNER
text: If thou spy’st any, run and bring me word;

speaker:
MASTER
               
              GUNNER
text: And thou shalt find me at the Governor’s.

speaker: BOY
text: Father, I warrant you, take you no care;

speaker: BOY
text: I’ll never trouble you if I may spy them.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Talbot, my life, my joy, again returned!

speaker: SALISBURY
text: How wert thou handled, being prisoner?

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Or by what means gott’st thou to be released?

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Discourse, I prithee, on this turret’s top.

speaker: TALBOT
text: The Duke of Bedford had a prisoner

speaker: TALBOT
text: Called the brave Lord Ponton de Santrailles;

speaker: TALBOT
text: For him was I exchanged and ransomèd.

speaker: TALBOT
text: But with a baser man-of-arms by far

speaker: TALBOT
text: Once in contempt they would have bartered me,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Which I disdaining, scorned, and cravèd death

speaker: TALBOT
text: Rather than I would be so vile-esteemed.

speaker: TALBOT
text: In fine, redeemed I was as I desired.

speaker: TALBOT
text: But O, the treacherous Fastolf wounds my heart,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Whom with my bare fists I would execute

speaker: TALBOT
text: If I now had him brought into my power.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Yet tell’st thou not how thou wert entertained.

speaker: TALBOT
text: With scoffs and scorns and contumelious taunts.

speaker: TALBOT
text: In open marketplace produced they me

speaker: TALBOT
text: To be a public spectacle to all.

speaker: TALBOT
text: “Here,” said they, “is the terror of the French,

speaker: TALBOT
text: The scarecrow that affrights our children so.”

speaker: TALBOT
text: Then broke I from the officers that led me,

speaker: TALBOT
text: And with my nails digged stones out of the ground

speaker: TALBOT
text: To hurl at the beholders of my shame.

speaker: TALBOT
text: My grisly countenance made others fly;

speaker: TALBOT
text: None durst come near for fear of sudden death.

speaker: TALBOT
text: In iron walls they deemed me not secure:

speaker: TALBOT
text: So great fear of my name ’mongst them were spread

speaker: TALBOT
text: That they supposed I could rend bars of steel

speaker: TALBOT
text: And spurn in pieces posts of adamant.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Wherefore a guard of chosen shot I had

speaker: TALBOT
text: That walked about me every minute-while;

speaker: TALBOT
text: And if I did but stir out of my bed,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Ready they were to shoot me to the heart.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: I grieve to hear what torments you endured,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: But we will be revenged sufficiently.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Now it is supper time in Orleance.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Here, through this grate, I count each one

speaker: SALISBURY
text: And view the Frenchmen how they fortify.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Let us look in; the sight will much delight thee.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Sir Thomas Gargrave and Sir William Glansdale,

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Let me have your express opinions

speaker: SALISBURY
text: Where is best place to make our batt’ry next?

speaker: GARGRAVE
text: I think at the north gate, for there stands lords.

speaker: GLANSDALE
text: And I, here, at the bulwark of the bridge.

speaker: TALBOT
text: For aught I see, this city must be famished

speaker: TALBOT
text: Or with light skirmishes enfeeblèd.

speaker: SALISBURY
text: O Lord, have mercy on us, wretched sinners!

speaker: GARGRAVE
text: O Lord, have mercy on me, woeful man!

speaker: TALBOT
text: What chance is this that suddenly hath crossed us?—

speaker: TALBOT
text: Speak, Salisbury—at least if thou canst, speak!

speaker: TALBOT
text: How far’st thou, mirror of all martial men?

speaker: TALBOT
text: One of thy eyes and thy cheek’s side struck off!—

speaker: TALBOT
text: Accursèd tower, accursèd fatal hand

speaker: TALBOT
text: That hath contrived this woeful tragedy!

speaker: TALBOT
text: In thirteen battles Salisbury o’ercame;

speaker: TALBOT
text: Henry the Fifth he first trained to the wars.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Whilst any trump did sound or drum struck up,

speaker: TALBOT
text: His sword did ne’er leave striking in the field.—

speaker: TALBOT
text: Yet liv’st thou, Salisbury? Though thy speech doth fail,

speaker: TALBOT
text: One eye thou hast to look to heaven for grace.

speaker: TALBOT
text: The sun with one eye vieweth all the world.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Heaven, be thou gracious to none alive

speaker: TALBOT
text: If Salisbury wants mercy at thy hands!—

speaker: TALBOT
text: Sir Thomas Gargrave, hast thou any life?

speaker: TALBOT
text: Speak unto Talbot. Nay, look up to him.—

speaker: TALBOT
text: Bear hence his body; I will help to bury it.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Salisbury, cheer thy spirit with this comfort,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Thou shalt not die whiles—

speaker: TALBOT
text: He beckons with his hand and smiles on me

speaker: TALBOT
text: As who should say “When I am dead and gone,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Remember to avenge me on the French.”

speaker: TALBOT
text: Plantagenet, I will; and, like thee, Nero,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Play on the lute, beholding the towns burn.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Wretched shall France be only in my name.

speaker: TALBOT
text: What stir is this? What tumult’s in the heavens?

speaker: TALBOT
text: Whence cometh this alarum and the noise?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: My lord, my lord, the French have gathered head.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: The Dauphin, with one Joan la Pucelle joined,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: A holy prophetess new risen up,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Is come with a great power to raise the siege.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Hear, hear, how dying Salisbury doth groan;

speaker: TALBOT
text: It irks his heart he cannot be revenged.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Frenchmen, I’ll be a Salisbury to you.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Pucelle or puzel, dauphin or dogfish,

speaker: TALBOT
text: Your hearts I’ll stamp out with my horse’s heels

speaker: TALBOT
text: And make a quagmire of your mingled brains.

speaker: TALBOT
text: Convey we Salisbury into his tent,

speaker: TALBOT
text: And then try what these dastard Frenchmen dare.
